attempted_job.description:
Which of the given formulas of PL is the best symbolization of the following sentence?
Doctors are helpful unless their patients are rude.
Find the speed of acompressionalwave in an iron rod whose specific gravity is 7.7 and whose Young's modulus is 27.5 × 10^6 1b/in^2.

This question refers to the following information.
Read the documents below.
Source 1
If then by the Use of Machines, the Manufacture of Cotton, an Article which we import, and are supplied with from other Countries, and which can everywhere be procured on equal Terms, has met with such amazing Success, may not greater Advantages be reasonably expected from cultivating to the utmost the Manufacture of Wool, the Produce of our own Island, an Article in Demand in all Countries, almost the universal Clothing of Mankind?
In the Manufacture of Woollens, the Scribbling Mill, the Spinning Frame, and the Fly Shuttle, have reduced manual Labour nearly One third, and each of them at its-first Introduction carried an Alarm to the Work People, yet each has contributed to advance the Wages and to increase the Trade, so that if an Attempt was now made to deprive us of the Use of them, there is no Doubt, but every Person engaged in the Business, would exert himself to defend them.
—Statement by the Cloth Merchants of Leeds, 1791
Source 2
Come, cropper lads of high renown,
Who love to drink good ale that's brown,
And strike each haughty tyrant down,
With hatchet, pike, and gun!
Oh, the cropper lads for me,
The gallant lads for me,
Who with lusty stroke,
The shear frames broke,
The cropper lads for me!
What though the specials still advance,
And soldiers nightly round us prance;
The cropper lads still lead the dance,
With hatchet, pike, and gun!
Oh, the cropper lads for me,
The gallant lads for me,
Who with lusty stroke
The shear frames broke,
The cropper lads for me!
—Luddite Song, The Cropper's Song, c. 1812

Which of the following statements are true concerning information criteria?

(i) Adjusted R-squared is an information criterion

(ii) If the residual sum of squares falls when an additional term is added, the value of the information criterion will fall

(iii) Akaike's information criterion always leads to model orders that are at least as large as those of Schwarz's information criterion

(iv) Akaike's information criterion is consistent
If an atom remains in an excited state for 10^-8 sec, what is the uncertainty in the energy of that state?
Electromagnetic radiation provides a means to probe aspects of the physical universe. Which of the following statements regarding radiation spectra is NOT correct?
options: All spectra are essentially the same for all elements.
The wavelengths identified in an absorption spectrum of an element are among those in its emission spectrum.
Spectral analysis can be used to identify the composition of galactic dust.
Spectral lines in the radio frequency region reveal the rotational structure of molecules.
Absorption and emission spectra of an element are always identical.
Lines in the infrared, visible, and ultraviolet regions of the spectrum reveal primarily the nuclear structure of the sample.
Emission spectra can be used to determine the temperature of a star.
X-ray spectroscopy is used primarily to identify the presence of biological molecules.
Spectral analysis can't determine the relative abundance of elements in a sample.
Absorption spectra can be used to determine which elements are present in distant stars.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Lines in the infrared, visible, and ultraviolet regions of the spectrum reveal primarily the nuclear structure of the sample.

attempted_job.description: Young Tina had never seen the space shuttle until her parents pointed out a picture of it ready to launch. When she next saw a picture of it flying, she had difficulty recognizing it. Which concept best explains this problem?
Suppose the government decides that in order to fight inflation, labor unions will not be allowed any wage increases until further notice. What type of inflation would this be aimed at?
A force of 100ntis required to stretch a steel wire 2.0mm^2 in cross-sectional area and 2.0 m long a distance of 0.50 mm. How much work is done?

It is known that a lab cart is moving east at 25 cm/s at time t1 = 0.10 s, and then moving east at 15 cm/s at t2 = 0.20 s. Is this enough information to determine the direction of the net force acting on the cart between t1 and t2?
options: No, because we don’t know if there are other forces acting on the cart apart from the net force.
No, because we don’t know whether forces such as friction or air resistance might be acting on the cart.
No, because we don't know the initial velocity of the cart.
Yes, since we know the cart keeps moving to the east, the net force must be in the direction of motion.
Yes, because the cart is moving in the east direction, the net force must be acting in the east direction.
No, because we don’t know the total distance covered by the cart.
Yes, since we know the cart is slowing down, its momentum change is opposite the direction of movement, and the net force is in the direction of momentum change.
No, because we don’t know the mass of the cart.
Yes, because the cart's velocity is decreasing, the net force must be acting in the west direction.
Yes, since the cart is slowing down, the net force must be acting in the opposite direction to the direction of motion.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Yes, since we know the cart is slowing down, its momentum change is opposite the direction of movement, and the net force is in the direction of momentum change.

attempted_job.description: Suppose the demand curve for oPads is given by $p=\frac{500-x}{10}, What is the elasticity value of this demand function.

Estimate the PEG ratio for a firm that has the following characteristics:
Length of high growth = five years
Growth rate in first five years = 25%
Payout ratio in first five years = 20%
Growth rate after five years = 8%
Payout ratio after five years = 50%
Beta = 1.0 
Risk-free rate = T-bond rate = 6%
Cost of equity = 6% + 1(5.5%) = 11.5%
Risk premium = 5.5%
What is the estimated PEG ratio for this firm?
What distinguishes the trade patterns of the Mesolithic from those of the Upper Paleolithic?
options: in the Mesolithic, goods traveled less far and fewer goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled farther but the same number of goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled less far but the same number of goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled the same distance but more goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled farther but fewer goods were exchanged
in the Upper Paleolithic, goods traveled less far but more goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled the same distance but fewer goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled less far but more goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled farther and more goods were exchanged
in the Mesolithic, goods traveled the same distance and the same number of goods were exchanged
attempted_job.correct_answer: in the Mesolithic, goods traveled less far but more goods were exchanged

attempted_job.description: Radiation from an X-ray source consists of two components of wavelengths $154.433 \mathrm{pm}$ and $154.051 \mathrm{pm}$. Calculate the difference in glancing angles $(2 \theta)$ of the diffraction lines arising from the two components in a diffraction pattern from planes of separation $77.8 \mathrm{pm}$.

The reaction of Br2(g) with Cl2(g) to form BrCl(g) has an equilibrium constant of 15.0 at a certain temperature. If 10.0 grams of BrCl is initially present in a 15.0-liter reaction vessel, what will the concentration of BrCl be at equilibrium?
Which of the following structures is located at the most central and protected part of the brain?
Which of the following statements is correct concerning the "reasonable person" standard in tort law?
options: The reasonable person standard applies only to people with a high level of education.
The reasonable person standard applies only to people without disabilities.
The reasonable person standard varies from person to person.
The reasonable person standard requires that a person act as would the average person in the community.
A person with a mental disability must act as would a person with the same mental disability.
A person with a physical disability must act as would a reasonable person with the same disability.
The reasonable person standard focuses on the defendant's subjective mental state rather than on the defendant's behavior
The reasonable person standard is based solely on the defendant's age.
A person with a mental disability is not subject to the reasonable person standard.
attempted_job.correct_answer: A person with a physical disability must act as would a reasonable person with the same disability.

attempted_job.description: The ground level of $\mathrm{Cl}$ is ${ }^2 \mathrm{P}_{3 / 2}$ and a ${ }^2 \mathrm{P}_{1 / 2}$ level lies $881 \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$ above it. Calculate the electronic contribution to the molar Gibbs energy of $\mathrm{Cl}$ atoms at  $500 \mathrm{~K}$.

options: Biased but inconsistent for one equation only
It is impossible to apply OLS to equations that are part of a simultaneous system
It is possible to apply OLS to equations that are part of a simultaneous system, but the results will be unpredictable

attempted_job.description: Using the following ionic radii, find the fractional void volume in (a)CsCl, (b)NaCl, (c)LiCl. Assume that the ions are hard spheres in contact. Ion Radius, \AA Li^+ 0.60 Na^+ 0.95 Cs^+ 1.69 Cl - 1.81 InCsClthe positive ions and the negative ions each comprise a simple cubic lattice. Each ion has eight oppositely charged ions as nearest neighbors. InNaCland LiClthe ions of each sign comprise a face-centered cubic lattice. Each ion has six oppositely charged ions as nearest neighbors.
options: 0.29 for CsCl, 0.35 for NaCl, 0.21 for LiCl
0.34 for CsCl, 0.28 for NaCl, 0.26 for LiCl
0.32 for CsCl, 0.31 for NaCl, 0.22 for LiCl
0.33 for CsCl, 0.29 for NaCl, 0.27 for LiCl
0.35 for CsCl, 0.30 for NaCl, 0.25 for LiCl
0.27 for CsCl, 0.33 for NaCl, 0.22 for LiCl
0.30 for CsCl, 0.33 for NaCl, 0.20 for LiCl
0.28 for CsCl, 0.34 for NaCl, 0.24 for LiCl
0.316 for CsCl, 0.32 for NaCl, 0.23 for LiCl
0.31 for CsCl, 0.27 for NaCl, 0.25 for LiCl
attempted_job.correct_answer: 0.316 for CsCl, 0.32 for NaCl, 0.23 for LiCl

attempted_job.description: Under a pure capitalist system, would the government play a majorrole in the economy?
options: Government intervenes only during economic crises to stabilize the market
Government plays a minor role by enforcing contracts and property rights
No government intervention in the economy
Government provides public goods and services but does not control private enterprise
Limited government intervention primarily in defense and public safety
Government sets minimum wage and workplace safety standards but is otherwise not involved
Government controls all aspects of the economy
Government regulates certain industries but does not control the economy
Government sets the prices for all goods and services
Government owns all businesses and properties
attempted_job.correct_answer: No government intervention in the economy

Electromagnetic radiation provides a means to probe aspects of the physical universe. Which of the following statements regarding radiation spectra is NOT correct?
options: All spectra are essentially the same for all elements.
The wavelengths identified in an absorption spectrum of an element are among those in its emission spectrum.
Spectral analysis can be used to identify the composition of galactic dust.
Spectral lines in the radio frequency region reveal the rotational structure of molecules.
Absorption and emission spectra of an element are always identical.
Lines in the infrared, visible, and ultraviolet regions of the spectrum reveal primarily the nuclear structure of the sample.
Emission spectra can be used to determine the temperature of a star.
X-ray spectroscopy is used primarily to identify the presence of biological molecules.
Spectral analysis can't determine the relative abundance of elements in a sample.

The gene frequency for theRhallele among American whites is 0.028. Among American blacks, the frequency is 0.446. Among East African blacks, the frequency of theRh allele is 0.630. Determine the degree of gene exchange (m) between the American whites and blacks.

A man, a woman, and their son went to their neighbor's house. The man intended to take back some tools that he believed were his and that the neighbor was keeping unlawfully. The woman believed that the tools were the man's, and she intended to help the man take them. When the son learned that the man and the woman were going to break into the neighbor's home, he decided to accompany them. The son planned to find some items inside that might be worth taking. Arriving at the neighbor's home, the man opened the front door, which was closed but unlocked. Upon entering, the son went to the neighbor's upstairs bedroom and found a watch, which he took. In the meantime, the man and the woman went to the garage and began rummaging through the neighbor's tools. The man found the tools, which he seized. The three of them then left the neighbor's home. In this jurisdiction, burglary is defined as the breaking and entering of any structure with the intent to commit a felony therein. Which, if any, individuals should be found guilty of conspiracy?
options: The man, the woman, and the son.
The woman, the son, and the neighbor.
The man, the son, and the neighbor.

attempted_job.description:
This question refers to the following information.
"At least one of the [world's] societies would have to somehow enormously increase its productivity [in order to achieve global hegemony]. That quantum jump would have to be made before the various scientific, technological, agricultural, and industrial revolutions on which our post-quantum-leap world rests. It could only be accomplished by exploiting the ecosystems, mineral resources, and human assets of whole continents outside the lands of the society making the jump. Western Europe did just that by means of its brutality and guns and, more important, by geographical and ecological luck."
Copyright © 2015 Cambridge University Press.
Alfred Crosby, historian, Ecological Imperialism, 2004
Based on your knowledge of world history, which of the following contributed LEAST to Western European global hegemony between 1450 and 1750 C.E.?
options: The exploitation of labor in colonized regions
The exploration of new sea routes to Asia and the Americas
The establishment of trading posts along Asian and African coasts
The exchange of food sources between the Americas and Europe
The spread of Christianity to non-European regions
The development and application of steam-powered technologies
The discovery and colonization of the Americas
The development of advanced naval technology
attempted_job.correct_answer: The development and application of steam-powered technologies

attempted_job.description: A 65-year-old woman presents with progressive muscle weakness that worsens throughout the day. She reports difficulty climbing stairs, brushing her hair, and speaking for extended periods. Symptoms improve with rest. She has recently experienced double vision and drooping eyelids, especially in the evening. Physical examination shows fatigable weakness of proximal muscles and ptosis. She has no sensory deficits. CT scan of the chest reveals a mass in the anterior mediastinum. Which of the following is the most likely anatomical site of the underlying pathology?
Arguing that someone couldn't have done something good because he is incapable of doing good commits the fallacy of
When a swimmer leaves cold water on a warm, breezy day, he experiences a cooling effect. Why?
options: The cold water causes a decrease in the swimmer's metabolic rate.
The swimmer feels cold due to the temperature difference.
Evaporation of water from the swimmer's skin cools the body down.
The swimmer feels cold due to the wind chill factor.
The swimmer's body is adapting to the water temperature after exiting.
The water removes heat from the swimmer's body.
The sun's rays are less effective on the swimmer's wet skin.
The water on the swimmer's body freezes.
The breeze makes the swimmer feel cold.
The swimmer's body temperature drops below normal.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The water removes heat from the swimmer's body.

attempted_job.description:
Under which of the following situations would bootstrapping be preferred to pure simulation?

i) If it is desired that the distributional properties of the data in the experiment

are the same as those of some actual data


ii) If it is desired that the distributional properties of the data in the experiment

are known exactly


iii) If the distributional properties of the actual data are unknown


iv) If the sample of actual data available is very small
Arrests may occasionally be made without a warrant. Which of the following is not one of those circumstances?
options: A misdemeanor has occurred, and an officer must believe that the person he has arrested committed it.
An officer must have probable cause that a felony has occurred and that the arrested person committed it.
An officer may arrest for a felony committed in his presence.
An officer may arrest for a misdemeanor that is a breach of peace and committed in arresting party's presence.
attempted_job.correct_answer: A misdemeanor has occurred, and an officer must believe that the person he has arrested committed it.

attempted_job.description: Within a cell, the amount of protein synthesized using a given mRNA molecule depends in part on which of the following?
options: The amount of protein synthesized is determined by the rate of cell division.
The age of the cell will determine how much protein is synthesized.
The amount of protein synthesized is determined by the amount of DNA replication.
The location and number of ribosomes in a cell will solely determine how much protein is synthesized.
The type of cell will solely determine how much protein is synthesized.
DNA methylation suppresses the expression of genes.
The size of the nucleus will determine how much protein is synthesized.
The number of chromosomes in a cell determines how much protein is synthesized.
Transcription factors mediate the binding of RNA polymerase and the initiation of transcription, which will determine how much protein is manufactured.
The speed with which mRNA is degraded will determine how much protein is synthesized.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The speed with which mRNA is degraded will determine how much protein is synthesized.

Use indirect truth tables to determine whether the following argument is valid. If the argument is invalid, choose an option which presents a counterexample. (There may be other counterexamples as well.)
(T · ~U) ⊃ ~W
~W ⊃ X
~Z ⊃ W
~(X · Z) / T ⊃ U
options: Invalid. Counterexample when T and X are true and U, W, and Z are false
Invalid. Counterexample when W, X, and Z are true and T and U are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, U, and X are true and W and Z are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, U, and W are true and X and Z are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, W, and Z are true and U and X are false

A Chord based distributed hash table (DHT) with 26 address space is used in a peer- to-peer file sharing network. There are currently 10 active peers in the network with node ID N1, N11, N15, N23, N31, N40, N45, N51, N60, and N63. Show all the target key (in ascending order, ignore the node's identifier itself) for N1.
List and compare the tissues that support and hold together theother tissues of the body.
options: Connective tissue is a single group of tissues that encapsulates organs without providing structural support.
The only function of connective tissue is to transport nutrients throughout the body.
Connective tissue is divided into two groups: bone and cartilage.
Connective tissue includes three types: muscle, nerve, and epithelial tissues.
Connective tissue is mainly composed of fluids and lacks solid structures like bone or cartilage.

Connective tissue functions to support and hold together structures of the body. They are classified into four groups by structure and/or function: bone, cartilage, blood, and fibrous connective tissue.
Connective tissue is categorized into five groups: bone, cartilage, blood, fibrous connective tissue, and adipose tissue.
Fibrous connective tissue doesn't contribute to the support and holding of other tissues.
Connective tissue only supports other tissues, it doesn't hold them together.
Connective tissues are not classified; all connective tissues have the same function and structure.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Connective tissue functions to support and hold together structures of the body. They are classified into four groups by structure and/or function: bone, cartilage, blood, and fibrous connective tissue.

This question refers to the following information.
Here is the case of a woman employed in the manufacturing department of a Broadway house. It stands for a hundred like her own. She averages three dollars a week. Pay is $1.50 for her room; for breakfast she has a cup of coffee; lunch she cannot afford. One meal a day is her allowance. This woman is young, she is pretty. She has "the world before her." Is it anything less than a miracle if she is guilty of nothing less than the "early and improvident marriage," against which moralists exclaim as one of the prolific causes of the distresses of the poor? Almost any door might seem to offer a welcome escape from such slavery as this. "I feel so much healthier since I got three square meals a day," said a lodger in one of the Girls' Homes. Two young sewing-girls came in seeking domestic service, so that they might get enough to eat. They had been only half-fed for some time, and starvation had driven them to the one door at which the pride of the American-born girl will not permit her to knock, though poverty be the price of her independence.
—Jacob Riis, How the Other Half Lives, 1890
The situation faced by the young women in the passage above is most directly comparable to which of the following?
options: Jewish immigrants in New York in the 1880s
Factory workers in the Industrial Revolution
Women suffragettes in the early 20th century

options: Government intervenes only during economic crises to stabilize the market

No government intervention in the economy
Government provides public goods and services but does not control private enterprise

Which of the following foramina pierce the sphenoid bone?
options: The foramen ovale, rotundum and spinosum
The foramen ovale, foramen spinosum and foramen lacerum
The foramen ovale, rotundum, spinosum and superior orbital fissure
The foramen ovale, rotundum, spinosum and carotid canal
The foramen ovale, rotundum and spinosum and foramen lacerum
The foramen ovale, rotundum, spinosum and foramen magnum
The foramen ovale and foramen spinosum

attempted_job.description: A monopsonist is identified by one of the following:
options: A wage payment lower than the average revenue product of labor.
Employment level lower than that of a competitive labor market.
A single buyer of labor services.
Employment level greater than that of a competitive labor market.
A wage payment lower than the marginal revenue product of labor.
A wage payment equal to that of a competitive labor market.
A wage payment higher than that of a competitive labor market.
A multiple seller of labor services.
Employment level equal to that of a competitive labor market.
A single seller of labor services.
attempted_job.correct_answer: A wage payment lower than the marginal revenue product of labor.

attempted_job.description: Which of the following rules regarding the interconversion of fuels are central to the integration of metabolic pathways?
options: Fatty acids can be made from and converted to carbohydrates and amino acids
Carbohydrates can be made from and converted to amino acids and fatty acids
Fatty acids can be made from but not converted to carbohydrates and amino acids
Amino acids can be made from and converted to fatty acids
attempted_job.correct_answer: Fatty acids can be made from but not converted to carbohydrates and amino acids

The patio area is a rectangle with dimensions 12 feet by 10 feet, and each individual brick is 4 inches by 6 inches by 2 inches. What would be the greatest number of bricks the salesman could sell to meet his sales criteria?

Studies into the etiology of Schizophrenia indicated a genetic predisposition to the condition but other factors are involved. From your knowledge of the subject identify which of the following statements is incorrect.
options: Excess dopamine or sensitivity to dopamine could be a contributory factor.
A lower rate of Schizophrenia diagnosed among Africa Americans.
Schizophrenia is primarily caused by poor lifestyle choices
Schizophrenia can be completely cured through psychotherapy
All schizophrenia patients exhibit severe personality splitting
attempted_job.correct_answer: A lower rate of Schizophrenia diagnosed among Africa Americans.

attempted_job.description: Heat is transferred to a cylinder having a volume of 5m^3 containing0.05 m^3 of saturated liquid water and 4.95 m^3 of saturatedwater vapor at 0.1MPa, until the cylinder is filled withsaturated vapor. Calculate the amount of heat transferredfor the process.

An automobile with a mass of $1000 \mathrm{~kg}$, including passengers, settles $1.0 \mathrm{~cm}$ closer to the road for every additional $100 \mathrm{~kg}$ of passengers. It is driven with a constant horizontal component of speed $20 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$ over a washboard road with sinusoidal bumps. The amplitude and wavelength of the sine curve are $5.0 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $20 \mathrm{~cm}$, respectively. The distance between the front and back wheels is $2.4 \mathrm{~m}$. Find the amplitude of oscillation of the automobile, assuming it moves vertically as an undamped driven harmonic oscillator. Neglect the mass of the wheels and springs and assume that the wheels are always in contact with the road.
Suppose a fluid particle moves according to the equations x = at, y =bt- ct^2, where a, b, c are constants. Find the normal and tangential accelerations.

This question refers to the following information.
"We have witnessed for more than a quarter of a century the struggles of the two great political parties for power and plunder, while grievous wrongs have been inflicted upon the suffering people. We charge that the controlling influences dominating both these parties have permitted the existing dreadful conditions to develop without serious effort to prevent or restrain them. Neither do they now promise us any substantial reform. They have agreed together to ignore, in the coming campaign, every issue but one. They propose to drown the outcries of a plundered people with the uproar of a sham battle over the tariff, so that capitalists, corporations, national banks, rings, trusts, watered stock, the demonetization of silver and the oppressions of the usurers may all be lost sight of. They propose to sacrifice our homes, lives, and children on the altar of mammon; to destroy the multitude in order to secure corruption funds from the millionaires.
"Assembled on the anniversary of the birthday of the nation, and filled with the spirit of the grand general and chief who established our independence, we seek to restore the government of the Republic to the hands of 'the plain people,' with which class it originated. We assert our purposes to be identical with the purposes of the National Constitution; to form a more perfect union and establish justice, insure domestic tranquillity, provide for the common defence, promote the general welfare, and secure the blessings of liberty for ourselves and our posterity."
Populist Party Platform, 1892
The sentiments expressed in the excerpt above about political parties are most similar to those expressed by
How many milliliters of 0.250 M KOH does it take to neutralize completely 50.0 mL of 0.150 M H3PO4?

A placebo-controlled clinical trial is conducted to assess whether a new antihypertensive drug is more effective than standard therapy. A total of 5000 patients with essential hypertension are enrolled and randomly assigned to one of two groups: 2500 patients receive the new drug and 2500 patients receive placebo. If the alpha is set at 0.01 instead of 0.05, which of the following is the most likely result?
options: The study will have less power
The study will require fewer participants
There is an increased likelihood of statistically significant findings
The study will be less reliable
Significant findings can be reported with greater confidence
The placebo effect will be more pronounced
The study will have more power
There is a decreased likelihood of statistically significant findings
attempted_job.correct_answer: Significant findings can be reported with greater confidence

attempted_job.description:
This question refers to the following information.
Read the following excerpt.
Never were talents of the highest genius of the most exalted kind, more profusely bestowed upon a human being. The genius of Napoleon is astounding. All branches of human knowledge seemed alike familiar to his gigantic mind. His conversations at St. Helena, scattered through the numerous and voluminous memorials of those who gleaned them, are replete with intensest interest. During the long agony of his imprisonment and his death, he conversed with perfect freedom upon the events of his marvelous career, and upon all those subjects or morals, politics, and religion, which most deeply concern the welfare of our race. There is no mind which will not be invigorated by familiarity with these profound thoughts, expressed with so much glow of feeling and energy of diction.
—John S. C. Abbott, historian, Napoleon at St. Helena, 1855
Which of the following features of the French empire under Napoleon does Abbott seem most impressed with?
options: Napoleon's influence on art and culture

attempted_job.description:
Use indirect truth tables to determine whether the following argument is valid. If the argument is invalid, choose an option which presents a counterexample. (There may be other counterexamples as well.)
(T · ~U) ⊃ ~W
~W ⊃ X
~Z ⊃ W
~(X · Z) / T ⊃ U
options: Invalid. Counterexample when T and X are true and U, W, and Z are false
Invalid. Counterexample when W, X, and Z are true and T and U are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, U, and X are true and W and Z are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, U, and W are true and X and Z are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, W, and Z are true and U and X are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, U, and Z are true and W and X are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, W, and X are true and U and Z are false
Invalid. Counterexample when U, W, and Z are true and T and X are false
Invalid. Counterexample when T, X, and Z are true and U and W are false

attempted_job.description: Alice owned Greyacre, a one-acre piece of property, in fee simple. Alice sold the property to Barbara who moved into the only residence on the land. Barbara neglected to record the deed, and three years later, Alice conveyed the same property to Charlene who immediately filed the deed she received from Alice. Greyacre's jurisdiction has a race-notice statute and a tract index system. If an action is filed for title to the property, the likely outcome would be for
options: Alice, since her ownership was first in time and she was of record.
Barbara, because she was the first to record her deed.
Alice, since she made two valid sales of the same property.
Charlene, because she was the first to record her deed.
Barbara, due to her physical possession of the property.
Barbara, because Charlene had notice of the prior conveyance between Alice and Barbara.
Charlene, because she relied on Alice's representation of ownership.
Alice, as she never actually transferred the property rights.
Charlene, as Barbara did not officially record her deed.
Charlene, because she was the last to receive the deed from Alice.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Barbara, because Charlene had notice of the prior conveyance between Alice and Barbara.

attempted_job.description: If $y_1$ and $y_2$ are a fundamental set of solutions of $t y^{\prime \prime}+2 y^{\prime}+t e^t y=0$ and if $W\left(y_1, y_2\right)(1)=2$, find the value of $W\left(y_1, y_2\right)(5)$.
The information that family members continuously exchange and that helps minimize deviation and maintain the family's stability is referred to as ________ feedback.
List the common types of registers used in the computer and statetheir purpose.
options: Program Counter, Instruction Register, Memory Address Register, Memory Buffer Register, Accumulator, General Purpose Registers, Index Register, Condition Code Register, Stack Pointer
Segment Register, Status Counter, Cache Pointer, Operation Register, Execution Register
Control Register, Status Register, Instruction Register, Program Counter
Base Register, Command Pointer, Logic Address Register, Accumulator Pointer, Buffer Status Register
Input Register, Output Register, Cache Register, Buffer Register, Data Register
Arithmetic Register, Logic Register, Data Counter, Command Register, Status Pointer
Memory Control Register, Instruction Buffer, Program Status Register, Data Counter, Input/Output Register
Stack Register, Queue Register, Instruction Register, Data Register, Buffer Register
Control Pointer, Address Buffer, Stack Register, Output Counter, Input Accumulator
Address Register, Control Accumulator, Program Logic Register, Execution Pointer, Stack Buffer Register
attempted_job.correct_answer: Program Counter, Instruction Register, Memory Address Register, Memory Buffer Register, Accumulator, General Purpose Registers, Index Register, Condition Code Register, Stack Pointer

attempted_job.description: List and compare the tissues that support and hold together theother tissues of the body.
options: Connective tissue is a single group of tissues that encapsulates organs without providing structural support.
The only function of connective tissue is to transport nutrients throughout the body.
Connective tissue is divided into two groups: bone and cartilage.
Connective tissue includes three types: muscle, nerve, and epithelial tissues.
Connective tissue is mainly composed of fluids and lacks solid structures like bone or cartilage.

Connective tissue functions to support and hold together structures of the body. They are classified into four groups by structure and/or function: bone, cartilage, blood, and fibrous connective tissue.
Connective tissue is categorized into five groups: bone, cartilage, blood, fibrous connective tissue, and adipose tissue.
Fibrous connective tissue doesn't contribute to the support and holding of other tissues.
Connective tissue only supports other tissues, it doesn't hold them together.
Connective tissues are not classified; all connective tissues have the same function and structure.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Connective tissue functions to support and hold together structures of the body. They are classified into four groups by structure and/or function: bone, cartilage, blood, and fibrous connective tissue.

attempted_job.description: Curtis stares at an image of a yellow star for a full minute. When he moves his gaze to a blank piece of white paper, he is likely to see an afterimage that is
Is the cumulative distribution function of the standard gaussian distribution $F(x)=1/\sqrt{2 \pi} \int_{-\infty}^x e^{-t^2/2} dt$ is log-concave?  Return 1 for yes and 0 for no.

Price of car: $5,779.00 Transportation charge: $73.00 Factory-installed equipment: Radio 95.50 Metallic paint 59.90 Racing stripes 39.50 Power steering 98.00 Wide radial tires 198.10 Air conditioning 429.00 Dealer-installed equipment: Mirror $8.50 Mats 10.75 Undercoat 35.00 Insurance : Collision ($100-deductible) $505.75 for two years Comprehensive 231.50 for two years Sales tax: 5% Cash deposit: $500.00 Cost of financing: 9(1/2)%per year for two Years Used car allowance: $370.00

A 20-month old clings nervously to her mother and does not want to explore a new play environment. She suddenly pushes her mother away and does not engage in any hugs her mother tries to give her. The infant’s facial expression shows anxiety but she looks away from her mother. The mother turns away from the baby as well. This child most likely has which type of attachment to her mother?

How long would it take a car, starting from rest and accelerating uniformly in a straight line at 5 m/s^2, to cover a distance of 200 m ?

A radioactive isotope, which is used in diagnostic imaging, has a half-life of 6.0 hours. If a quantity of this isotope has an activity of 150 mCi when it is delivered to a hospital, how much activity will remain 24 hours after delivery? (mCi = microcuries)

An air-vapor mixture, initially at 60°F and 55 percent relative humidity, is heated until its temperature reaches 85°F. Determine (a) the initial and final specifichumidities, (b) the final relative humidity, and (c) the amount of heat added perlbmof dry air. Assume the mixture to be at a pressure of 14.7psia.
options: Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00802lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 22.64 percent, Heat added: 5.1 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00602lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 23.64 percent, Heat added: 6.1 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial specific humidity: 0.00602lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final specific humidity: 0.00502lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 26.50 percent, Heat added: 6.5 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial specific humidity: 0.00752lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final specific humidity: 0.00702lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 27.50 percent, Heat added: 7.4 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial specific humidity: 0.00802lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final specific humidity: 0.00902lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 21.64 percent, Heat added: 8.1 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial specific humidity: 0.00702lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final specific humidity: 0.00852lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 20.64 percent, Heat added: 7.8 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00652lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 25.14 percent, Heat added: 6.0 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00702lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 24.64 percent, Heat added: 7.1 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00652lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 23.14 percent, Heat added: 6.6 Btu/lbm of dry air
Initial specific humidity: 0.00502lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final specific humidity: 0.00602lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 28.64 percent, Heat added: 5.5 Btu/lbm of dry air
attempted_job.correct_answer: Initial and final specific humidities: 0.00602lbm vapor/lbm dry air, Final relative humidity: 23.64 percent, Heat added: 6.1 Btu/lbm of dry air

attempted_job.description: A Gaussian filter with \vertH(\omega)\vert^2 = e^-(\omega)2 is used to filter noise at the output of an amplifier stage. Determine the bandwidth of the filter.
When a swimmer leaves cold water on a warm, breezy day, he experiences a cooling effect. Why?
options: The cold water causes a decrease in the swimmer's metabolic rate.
The swimmer feels cold due to the temperature difference.
Evaporation of water from the swimmer's skin cools the body down.
The swimmer feels cold due to the wind chill factor.
The swimmer's body is adapting to the water temperature after exiting.
The water removes heat from the swimmer's body.
The sun's rays are less effective on the swimmer's wet skin.
The water on the swimmer's body freezes.
The breeze makes the swimmer feel cold.
The swimmer's body temperature drops below normal.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The water removes heat from the swimmer's body.

You add radioactive material whose activity per unit mass was 45.5 s^-1 g^-1 to a liquid and then dilute it so the activity per unit mass was 6.5 s^-1 g^-1 . What is the dilution factor and the original amount of radioactive material? Assume 100 mg of radioactive material was added.
options: Dilution factor is 4.0, Original amount of radioactive material is 300 mg
Dilution factor is 9.0, Original amount of radioactive material is 800 mg
Dilution factor is 10.0, Original amount of radioactive material is 900 mg
Dilution factor is 8.0, Original amount of radioactive material is 700 mg
Dilution factor is 6.5, Original amount of radioactive material is 550 mg
Dilution factor is 7.0, Original amount of radioactive material is 600 mg
Dilution factor is 5.5, Original amount of radioactive material is 450 mg
Dilution factor is 5.0, Original amount of radioactive material is 400 mg
Dilution factor is 7.5, Original amount of radioactive material is 650 mg
Dilution factor is 6.0, Original amount of radioactive material is 500 mg
attempted_job.correct_answer: Dilution factor is 7.0, Original amount of radioactive material is 600 mg

attempted_job.description: Oil (sp. gr. = 0.96, viscosity \mu = 50 cp) flows past a flat plate with a free-stream velocity of 1.1 ft/s. What is the thickness of the boundary layer, and the displacement thickness 6 inches from the leading edge?
options: Boundary layer thickness is 0.50 inches and displacement thickness is 0.15 inches
Boundary layer thickness is 1.25 inches and displacement thickness is 0.50 inches
Boundary layer thickness is 0.75 inches and displacement thickness is 0.25 inches
Boundary layer thickness is 0.90 inches and displacement thickness is 0.333 inches
Boundary layer thickness is 1.15 inches and displacement thickness is 0.45 inches
Boundary layer thickness is 1.0 inches and displacement thickness is 0.4 inches
Boundary layer thickness is 0.65 inches and displacement thickness is 0.20 inches
Boundary layer thickness is 0.80 inches and displacement thickness is 0.30 inches
Boundary layer thickness is 0.85 inches and displacement thickness is 0.28 inches
Boundary layer thickness is 0.95 inches and displacement thickness is 0.38 inches
attempted_job.correct_answer: Boundary layer thickness is 0.90 inches and displacement thickness is 0.333 inches

attempted_job.description: Dr. Delbert Dinwoody is currently treating a 35-year-old client who has received a diagnosis of Alcohol Dependence. With the client's permission, Dr. Dinwoody invites the client's wife to participate in couple therapy. Inviting the wife to participate in therapy is:
options: acceptable as long as the client and wife are not separated or in the process of divorce.
acceptable as long as the wife is not receiving mental health services from another professional.
"unacceptable because in this situation, the wife is “vulnerable to undue inﬂuence."""
acceptable only if the wife has also been diagnosed with Alcohol Dependence.
acceptable as long as he believes couple therapy will benefit the client's progress in dealing with his alcohol problem.
unacceptable because the wife should seek her own professional help.
"unacceptable because doing so creates a ""conﬂict of interest.”"
acceptable only if the wife gives her written consent to participate in the therapy.
unacceptable because it may create a bias in the therapist's approach towards the client.
unacceptable because it infringes on the client's individual therapy process.
attempted_job.correct_answer: acceptable as long as he believes couple therapy will benefit the client's progress in dealing with his alcohol problem.

Why do economists make seasonal adjustmentsin economic data before trying to isolate long-term trends?
options: To differentiate between urban and rural economic activity
To gain a more accurate picture of the actual trend
To adjust for variations in weather patterns
To account for short-term market fluctuations
To prepare for upcoming financial crises
To correct for data collection errors
To align with fiscal policy changes
attempted_job.correct_answer: To gain a more accurate picture of the actual trend

attempted_job.description: John divided his souvenir hat pins into two piles. The two piles had an equal number of pins. He gave his brother one-half of one-third of one pile. John had 66 pins left. How many pins did John originally have?
Mill claims that the principle of utility:
options: can only be proven through extensive observation and study.
can be proven from principles that are known empirically.
can be proven, but only under certain conditions.
is inherently flawed and therefore cannot be proven.
is a theory that has been widely disproven.
can be proven, but this is unique to the principle of utility.
cannot be proven, but this is common to all first principles.
can be proven, but only by using a complex mathematical model.
cannot be proven, and this is a unique problem for the theory.
can be proven from self-evident principles.
attempted_job.correct_answer: cannot be proven, but this is common to all first principles.
